text: I feel a sense of quiet anticipation here. The modern, clean lines and transparent glass of the meeting room invite me to ponder the purpose of gathering—do people collaborate better when they can see each other clearly, unobstructed? Yet, the space is currently empty, filled only with the soft whir of distant air conditioning and the expectation of future discussion. It is as if the room itself is waiting, holding its breath until ideas begin to flow within.
